text.title: Issuance Flow and Per-Call Verification
text.summary: Slack reactji can prompt the approval but only the signed callback satisfies the gate; verification runs at every tool call, not at session start.

section.title: Initiate via chat, redeem via signed callback
section.p: The chat surface is convenient and audit-poor. Use it to trigger the approval workflow but require the human to redeem an OAuth-style callback that issues the JWT signed by the approval service's KMS key.

rule.statement: The agent MUST request approval via a workflow that posts a unique URL to a chat thread; the URL MUST require the approver's SSO/IdP login, MUST display the exact `tool`, `target`, and `args_hash` before signing, and MUST issue a JWT whose `sub` matches the SSO subject. The agent MUST present the JWT on the tool call; verification MUST happen at the policy enforcement point, NOT cached in the agent's memory. `jti` MUST be recorded in a tamper-evident store (signed append-only log or HSM-backed DB); replay attempts MUST be denied AND alerted. Approvals MUST NOT be self-issued by the agent's identity, and the approver's identity MUST NOT match `aud` (no self-approval). All denials and approvals MUST be retained per the org's compliance window.
rule.rationale: Chat surfaces are mutable and unsigned; SSO + KMS-signed callback turns a 3-second human action into a non-repudiable, auditable artifact that satisfies SOC2 CC6 and EU AI Act post-deployment monitoring.

example.code: def request_approval(tool, target, args): args_hash = sha256(canonical_json(args)) url = approve_svc.start(tool, target, args_hash, ttl=300) slack.post(channel=incident.channel, text=f"Approve `{tool}` on `{target}`? {url}") token = approve_svc.wait_for_callback(timeout=300) return verify(token, audience=AGENT_ID, expected_scope={"tool": tool, "target": target, "args_hash": args_hash})
example.note: Args hash binds the token to the exact call; verification re-checks every claim at use time.
Boolean cache loses scope binding entirely; one approval becomes a session-wide write capability.

antipattern.description: The agent's own service account both issues and consumes the approval token.
antipattern.reason: Self-approval erases the human-in-the-loop; an audit cannot distinguish autonomous action from approved action.

section.reference: Reference verifier with signature, scope, expiry, replay checks.
